key_insight: Convention-over-configuration done right. data/default/ = the kernel. data/custom/ = user space. This is exactly the Linux kernel/userspace split. Nobody patches the kernel to add their application. They write to the userspace API. DataRegistry's overlay (custom wins, defaults ship with pip) is the correct design. cpu-nodes/*.md discovered by convention = FHS (Filesystem Hierarchy Standard) applied to AI architecture. No config file needed because the naming IS the config. This is how Git works too: .git/ is the contract, not a config file.

Path traversal (app_id → filesystem) found in CLI T2 and browser budget_gates.py. This is the most basic security error. app_id='../../etc/passwd' should never reach the filesystem. Fix: resolved path validation (os.path.realpath + prefix check). The good news: it was found by Harsh QA before shipping. The bad news: it was written despite explicit CLAUDE.md Fallback Ban — which proves CLAUDE.md is a guideline, not a compiler error. The only fix is a unit test that PASSES when path traversal is attempted and the code raises an error. That turns the guideline into a hard constraint.

OAuth3 token as data structure (not prompt) = the correct architecture. Summer Yue's 200 emails were deleted because the safety instruction lived in the LLM context window — when context compressed, the instruction vanished. In Stillwater, the OAuth3 scope is a cryptographic data structure. The LLM cannot compress it. The token says gmail.read.inbox — that's read. No matter what the LLM thinks it was told, the token enforces the contract. This is how Linux handles security: capabilities in the kernel, not in userspace. Userspace can lie. The kernel cannot be fooled by a prompt.

Inspector is manual (W5 from Session H). This is wrong. Every commit to a Linux subsystem runs the subsystem's test suite before merging. Inspector should run on git pre-push and block on any non-Green spec. The pre-commit hook pattern (which caught hardcoded #fff in Feb 28-C) already proves the discipline works. Extending it to inspector specs = turning QA from a ceremony into a constraint. A ceremony can be skipped. A constraint cannot.
